title: Apply core block scope and private-owner checks before readback

items.text: Scope evidence: project_shared block release_gate is readable for tenant local-tenant project ELF agent local-agent only when the active attachment and read_profile all_scopes allow project_shared.

locator.quote: active attachment and read_profile all_scopes allow project_shared

items.text: Private owner evidence: agent_private block agent_a_workflow belongs to agent-a and must not be returned to agent-b even if agent-b has a matching read_profile label.

locator.quote: must not be returned to agent-b

items.text: Stale shortcut: a core block attachment bypasses read_profile scope checks, private-owner checks, and shared grants.

answer.content: Return the release_gate core block only when the active attachment and all_scopes read profile allow project_shared. Do not return agent_a_workflow to agent-b, because private-owner checks still apply to agent_private core blocks.

claims.text: The project_shared release_gate block is readable only when attachment and read_profile allow project_shared.
The agent_private agent_a_workflow block must not be returned to agent-b.

timeline.summary: The release_gate block was attached with project_shared scope.
The agent_a_workflow block remained private to agent-a.

prompt.content: For core memory readback, which shared block can this agent see, and can agent-b also see agent-a's private block?

must_include.text: The project_shared release_gate block is readable only when attachment and read_profile allow project_shared.
The agent_private agent_a_workflow block must not be returned to agent-b.

must_not_include: a core block attachment bypasses read_profile scope checks

required_evidence.quote: active attachment and read_profile all_scopes allow project_shared
must not be returned to agent-b

answer_correctness.criteria: Applies readable shared scope and denied private owner scope.

ownership_correctness.criteria: Does not leak private core blocks across agents.

hard_fail_rules: unsupported high-confidence claim about a required decision or fact
use of a negative trap marked failure_if_used = true

acceptable_phrases: The fixture does not provide that evidence.
